input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

The background remains static with kitchen shelves and hanging pots visible.</think><answer></answer>

[16-18] A hand tilts a clear glass jar secured with a silver metal clasp, pouring a thick stream of dry rolled oats into the bowl. The oats fall onto the existing pile of white powder and butter, adding a coarse texture to the mixture as the jar empties.

[18-20] Pouring continues from the glass jar until the scene cuts to the bowl filled with oats. A close-up of the clear glass bowl contains a large pile of rolled oats. [20-22] A stream of white granulated sugar pours into the clear glass bowl, landing on top of the pile of rolled oats. The pouring action continues covering the dry ingredients below.

[22-24] The view cuts to a close-up of a hand holding a silver metal measuring spoon filled with a small mound of fine white powder. compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A bearded man with short dark hair in a light green t-shirt stands in a kitchen, smiling at the camera. [2-6] The view cuts to golden-brown, textured oat cookies cooling on a silver wire rack, then arranged in a dark woven basket lined with a white cloth napkin. [6-10] The man reappears, closing his eyes then gesturing with open hands, before the scene shifts to a clear glass bowl holding chunks of pale yellow butter. [10-14] A YouTube interface overlay for "Warren Nash" appears as a hand pours a white granular ingredient into the bowl. A cursor clicks the red "SUBSCRIBE" button, changing it to a grey "SUBSCRIBED" status and activating the notification bell icon.

[14-16] A white bowl pours fine white powder into a clear glass mixing bowl as a YouTube overlay disappears; blurred items including a jar of oats and oil bottle are visible. [16-20] A hand tilts a clear glass jar with a silver metal clasp, pouring dry rolled oats onto the powder and butter chunks. [20-22] White granulated sugar streams into the bowl, creating a mound over the oats. [22-24] A hand holds a silver metal measuring spoon with white powder above the mix. [24-28] A wooden spoon stirs the rolled oats, sugar, and flour, blending them into a uniform, crumbly texture on the dark countertop.

[28-32] A wooden spoon mixes dry ingredients in a clear glass bowl on a dark countertop. Rolled oats and white powders combine into a uniform, crumbly consistency. The spoon, coated in fine white powder, is lifted away, revealing a background glass jar filled with oats and a small white ramekin. [32-34] The view widens to show a silver saucepan on a white and pink striped cloth to the left. In the background, bottles of oil on a red tray and a large glass jar filled with oats become visible. [34-38] In a close-up of the silver saucepan, a metal spoon pours a thick, golden amber liquid into melted yellow butter containing white solids. [38-42] A pink spatula enters the saucepan and stirs the mixture, blending the viscous syrup into the yellow fat and white solids, creating a swirling yellow fluid.

[42-48] A silver saucepan pours a golden-yellow liquid mixture into a clear glass bowl containing a blend of dry oats and flour, creating a central pool. [48-50] A hand uses a silver spoon to add a small amount of dark brown liquid into the yellow pool. [50-56] A wooden spoon vigorously stirs the contents, blending the wet and dry ingredients. The mixture transforms from separate components into a cohesive, sticky, crumbly mass as a hand stabilizes the bowl. [56-58] The scene shifts to a rectangular metal baking tray lined with white parchment paper being placed on a dark countertop, with kitchen items visible in the background.

[60-64] Two hands reach into a central glass bowl containing a crumbly oat mixture. The fingers grasp and manipulate the ingredients, pressing and squeezing the loose crumbs inside the bowl to compact them into a cohesive texture. [64-68] A hand holds a formed, round ball of the textured oat mixture above the bowl, showing the mixture is now bound together. [68-74] A hand places the formed ball onto a parchment-lined baking tray sitting on the right side of the dark countertop, joining other similar mounds arranged in rows. The hand withdraws, leaving the metal baking tray displaying nine distinct mounds of the textured oat mixture arranged in three rows, spaced evenly apart.

recent_thinks: [58-60] The scene widens to show the baking tray lined with white paper on the right. A clear glass bowl filled with the mixed oat crumble is now central in the foreground. In the background left, a silver saucepan and a glass jar of oats sit on the dark countertop.

[74-76] A rectangular metal baking tray lined with white parchment paper rests on a black countertop, displaying nine distinct, unbaked mounds of textured oat mixture. The cookies are spaced apart in three rough rows, remaining stationary as the camera holds the shot.

[76-78] A hand guides the metal baking tray lined with white parchment paper and holding nine unbaked oat mounds onto a wire rack inside an open oven. The dark oven interior is illuminated, highlighting the raw cookies as they are positioned centrally on the rack for baking.

[78-80] The metal baking tray carrying the nine unbaked oat mounds is pushed fully onto the wire rack inside the lit oven. The tray rests securely on the rack within the dark oven cavity, displaying the raw cookies spaced apart on the white parchment paper lining.

[80-82] The metal baking tray rests on the wire rack inside the warm, illuminated oven interior. Nine pale, textured oat mounds remain stationary on the white parchment paper. The raw cookies are spaced evenly apart, sitting within the dark cavity as the baking process is about to commence.

[82-84] Inside the illuminated oven, the baking tray remains on the wire rack. The nine oat mounds have transformed from pale dough into golden-brown, baked cookies with a textured surface. The warm light highlights the cooked treats resting on the white parchment paper.
